chore(beads): Update issue timestamps for in-progress tasks

Issue Tracking Updates:
- bd-3e1t.7.8 (Config: SAT thresholds + feature flags): Updated timestamp
  reflecting continued work on tile-skip configuration and feature flags
- bd-3e1t.8.3 (Integrate strategy selector + evidence log): Updated timestamp
  reflecting ongoing integration of the Bayesian diff-strategy selector
  with evidence logging and safety guards

Both tasks are part of the larger effort to implement adaptive diff
strategy selection using Beta-Binomial estimators with configurable
hysteresis and safety guards for stable, deterministic rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-21hz","title":"Widget API Frame Integration Tests","description":"## Purpose

Create comprehensive integration tests for the Widget + Frame API to ensure all Frame features work correctly.

## Test Categories

### 1. Frame Feature Tests
- `frame.buffer` access from Widget::render
- `frame.hit_grid` registration and retrieval
- `frame.cursor_position` setting and clearing
- `frame.degradation` level checking

### 2. Widget Category Tests
Each category should have tests:

**Category A (buffer-only):**
- Block renders borders correctly
- Paragraph wraps text
- Rule draws lines

**Category B (hit testing):**
- List registers hit regions for each item
- Table registers hit regions for rows/cells
- Hit IDs are unique and retrievable

**Category C (cursor):**
- TextInput positions cursor correctly
- Cursor visibility toggles work

**Category D (degradation):**
- ProgressBar simplifies at EssentialOnly
- Spinner shows label-only at reduced levels

### 3. Edge Cases
- Empty area (0x0)
- Single cell area (1x1)
- Unicode content with Frame
- Widget nesting with Frame forwarding

## Test Template

```rust
#[test]
fn test_widget_frame_feature() {
    let mut pool = GraphemePool::new();
    let mut frame = Frame::new(80, 24, &mut pool);
    
    // Arrange
    let widget = TestWidget::new();
    let area = Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5);
    
    // Act
    widget.render(area, &mut frame);
    
    // Assert
    assert!(frame.buffer.get(0, 0).symbol == "expected");
    // Add detailed assertions...
}
```

## Success Criteria
- All Frame features have dedicated tests
- Each widget category has test coverage
- Edge cases documented and tested
- Tests include logging for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:36:31.522608704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:44:43.884308586Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:44:43.884289249Z","close_reason":"Added Frame integration tests for Frame + widget features","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-22fb","title":"Virtualized List: Fenwick Tree Cache Layout + Batch Updates","description":"## Goal
Make prefix-sum updates and queries cache-friendly and fast under large lists and frequent edits.

## Core Idea (Extreme Optimization)
- Store Fenwick tree in a contiguous Vec with power-of-two alignment.\n- Use batch update API to apply multiple delta updates in a single pass.\n- Optional: maintain a sparse dirty list and rebuild tree when density crosses threshold.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Update throughput improves >= 2x over naive per-item updates on 10k items.\n- Query latency p95 < 5us for 100k items.\n- No allocations in steady-state operations.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_batch_update_equivalence: batch updates match sequential updates.\n- property_prefix_sum_correct: random updates preserve prefix sums.\n- perf_fenwick_hotpath: benchmark p50/p95; log results.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Scroll + update storm; log update throughput + query latency.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:13:30.086116846Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:57:14.268652759Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:57:14.268631560Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-22q","title":"Implement Theme system with semantic color slots","description":"PHASE 4 - THEME SYSTEM

## Background
A Theme provides semantic color slots that map to actual colors. This enables consistent styling and easy theme switching (light/dark mode, custom themes).

## Technical Requirements

### Semantic Color Slots
pub struct Theme {
    // Primary UI
    pub primary: AdaptiveColor,
    pub secondary: AdaptiveColor,
    pub accent: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Backgrounds
    pub background: AdaptiveColor,
    pub surface: AdaptiveColor,
    pub overlay: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Text
    pub text: AdaptiveColor,
    pub text_muted: AdaptiveColor,
    pub text_subtle: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Semantic
    pub success: AdaptiveColor,
    pub warning: AdaptiveColor,
    pub error: AdaptiveColor,
    pub info: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Borders
    pub border: AdaptiveColor,
    pub border_focused: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Selection
    pub selection_bg: AdaptiveColor,
    pub selection_fg: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Scrollbar
    pub scrollbar_track: AdaptiveColor,
    pub scrollbar_thumb: AdaptiveColor,
}

### Adaptive Colors
pub enum AdaptiveColor {
    Fixed(Color),
    Adaptive { light: Color, dark: Color },
}

impl AdaptiveColor {
    pub fn resolve(&self, is_dark: bool) -> Color {
        match self {
            Self::Fixed(c) => *c,
            Self::Adaptive { light, dark } => {
                if is_dark { *dark } else { *light }
            }
        }
    }
}

### Built-in Theme Presets
pub mod themes {
    pub fn default() -> Theme;      // Sensible defaults
    pub fn dark() -> Theme;         // Dark mode
    pub fn light() -> Theme;        // Light mode
    pub fn nord() -> Theme;         // Nord color scheme
    pub fn dracula() -> Theme;      // Dracula colors
    pub fn solarized_dark() -> Theme;
    pub fn solarized_light() -> Theme;
}

### Theme Detection
impl Theme {
    /// Detect dark mode from environment/terminal
    pub fn detect_dark_mode() -> bool {
        // Check COLORFGBG for light/dark background
        // Check OSC 11 background query (if supported)
        // Default: assume dark
    }
}

### Theme Resolution
Widgets reference theme colors:
  let style = Style::new().fg(theme.text).bg(theme.surface);

### Runtime Theme Switching
- Theme stored in Context or Model
- All widgets receive theme reference
- Switching triggers full redraw

## Tests Required
- [ ] All semantic slots have sensible defaults
- [ ] Adaptive color resolution correct
- [ ] Built-in presets complete
- [ ] Dark mode detection works
- [ ] Theme switching triggers redraw

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ semantic color slots
- [ ] Light/dark adaptive colors
- [ ] 5+ built-in presets
- [ ] Detection heuristics
- [ ] Theme switching API

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.6","title":"Dirty-span tracking for sparse diff (interval union)","description":"Background: diff benches show compute_dirty is often ~equal to compute for 1-row and ~5% sparse edits at 200x60 and 240x80; dirty rows are too coarse and still scan full rows. Goal: track per-row dirty spans (interval union) so diff scans only changed ranges while preserving exact output. Scope: add span storage to Buffer, merge policy, overflow fallback to full-row scan, and deterministic scan order identical to current row-major changes list. Constraints: no unsafe; no per-cell allocations; span storage O(rows)+O(spans); maintain dirty-soundness (if any cell in row y changed, at least one span covers it); must not change observable rendering output. Logging: emit evidence log fields for span_count, span_coverage, fallback_reason, and scan_cost_estimate. Tests: unit tests for merge/overflow and randomized diff-equivalence; property-based tests for edge cases (u16::MAX, empty rows, full rows).  {"id":"bd-3e1t.6.1","title":"Spec: dirty-span model, invariants, and merge policy","description":"Background: dirty-row tracking is too coarse for sparse updates. Task: specify per-row span data model, merge/union policy, max span cap, overflow fallback to full-row scan, deterministic ordering, and reset semantics via clear_dirty. Considerations: scissor/opacity interactions, optional guard bands, config defaults, and evidence log fields. Output: written spec captured in notes.","notes":"Dirty-Span Model (Spec v1)

Motivation
- Dirty-row tracking is too coarse for sparse updates: scanning full rows wastes work.
- Dirty-span tracking records per-row modified x-ranges to reduce diff scan cost while preserving soundness.

Data Model
- For each row y, maintain a small ordered list of spans: Span { x0: u16, x1: u16 } half-open.
- Spans are non-overlapping, sorted by x0 ascending.
- Per-row full_row/overflow flag indicates the row should be scanned fully; when true, spans list is empty.
- Optional guard band (merge_gap) merges spans that are within ≤ merge_gap cells.

Invariants (Must Hold)
1) Soundness: for any cell (x,y) mutated since last clear, ∃ span in row y such that x ∈ [x0,x1).
2) Sorted + non-overlapping spans per row (after merge).
3) Deterministic merge order: given the same mutation sequence, span lists are identical.
4) Full-row flag dominates: if full_row[y] = true, spans[y] is empty and row is scanned fully.

Merge / Union Policy
- Insert span (x0,x1) into row y:
  - If full_row[y] => no-op (already full).
  - Insert at position by x0 (binary search recommended).
  - Merge with neighbors when overlaps or when gap ≤ merge_gap.
  - After merge, if span_count > max_spans_per_row => set full_row[y]=true and clear spans.
- Default implementation values (current): merge_gap = 1, max_spans_per_row = 64.
- Optional future: make these configurable; add coverage threshold to flip to full_row.

Mutation API (Conceptual)
- mark_dirty_span(y, x0, x1)
- mark_dirty_cell(y, x) => mark_dirty_span(y, x, x+1)
- mark_dirty_rect(rect): for each row in rect, call mark_dirty_span(y, rect.x, rect.right())

Reset Semantics
- clear_dirty_spans(): clears all spans and full_row flags.
- On Buffer::new, initialize all rows as full_row to guarantee first diff is full.

Interaction with Existing Dirty Rows
- Dirty-span is a strict refinement; dirty_rows is a coarse fallback.
- If both exist, diff strategy may choose:
  - full_row[y] => full scan (row-skip still allowed)
  - spans[y].is_empty() => skip row
  - spans[y] => scan only spans
- False positives are allowed; false negatives are forbidden.

Scissor / Opacity / Compositing
- Only record spans for actual writes that pass scissor checks.
- Opacity compositing may produce no net change, but still mark span; false positives are OK.
- When scissor stacks are used, mark spans only for the intersected row segments.

Determinism & Ordering
- Use Vec/SmallVec per row to avoid hash-based ordering.
- Merge order is deterministic: left-to-right; stable tie-breakers for equal x0.

Evidence / Telemetry Fields (proposed)
- dirty_span_rows, dirty_span_total, dirty_span_overflows
- max_spans_per_row, merge_gap, span_coverage_cells
- rows_full_dirty

Failure Modes + Fallbacks
- Excessive fragmentation => full_row fallback
- Span list overflow => full_row fallback
- Resize => re-init to full dirty

Tests (Spec-level)
- Unit: merge behavior (overlap, adjacency, guard band, overflow)
- Property: soundness vs random mutation sets (spans cover all mutated cells)
- Regression: deterministic output for identical mutation sequences
- E2E: span stats logged in JSONL under fixed seed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:46.856751717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:30:27.225108113Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:16:10.481846217Z","close_reason":"Completed dirty-span spec in docs/spec/state-machines.md section 3.14","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:46.856751717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.8.1","title":"Spec: Beta-Binomial estimator + cost model","description":"Background: adaptive selection needs a principled estimator and cost model. Task: define Beta-Binomial prior and update rule, confidence metric, cost models for compute, dirty rows, spans, and SAT, hysteresis to avoid thrash, deterministic tie-breaks, and config defaults. Output: spec in notes.","notes":"## Spec: Beta-Binomial estimator + cost model (bd-3e1t.8.1)

### Objective
Define the estimator, cost models, hysteresis, and deterministic tie-breaks for selecting among diff strategies (Full, DirtyRows, Spans, SAT, FullRedraw) with a Beta-Binomial change-rate model.

### Estimator (change-rate p)
- Model: p ~ Beta(α, β), p = fraction of cells changed.
- Prior defaults: α0=1, β0=19 (E[p]=0.05).
- Decay: α <- α * decay + k, β <- β * decay + (n - k), decay=0.95.
- Observation:
  - n = cells scanned by the selected diff path in the last frame.
  - k = cells changed (size of diff output).
  - If n < min_observation_cells, skip update (default 0).
- Bounds: clamp α, β to [1e-6, 1e6] for stability.

### Confidence metric
- Use posterior variance Var[p] = αβ / ((α+β)^2 (α+β+1)).
- Optionally compute q95 via Beta quantile for conservative mode.
- Define uncertainty = sqrt(Var[p]); treat as "high" if > 0.05 (default).

### Strategy cost models
Let:
- N = total cells (W * H)
- D = dirty rows
- W = width
- p_hat = E[p] (or q95 if conservative)
- k = changed cells (k ≈ p_hat * N)

1) Full (row-skip):
- Cost_full = c_row * H + c_scan * D * W + c_emit * k

2) DirtyRows:
- Cost_dirty = c_scan * D * W + c_emit * k

3) Spans (interval union per row):
- Let S = total scanned span cells (sum of span lengths).
- Cost_span = c_scan * S + c_emit * k + c_span_overhead
- Default c_span_overhead = c_row * D (row bookkeeping).

4) SAT / tile-skip:
- Let T = tile count, T_dirty = dirty tiles, C_dirty = cells in dirty tiles.
- Cost_sat = c_sat_build * N + c_tile * T + c_scan * C_dirty + c_emit * k
- Default: c_sat_build = c_scan (one pass), c_tile = 0.02 * c_scan.

5) FullRedraw:
- Cost_redraw = c_emit * N

### Decision rule
- Compute expected cost for each available strategy.
- Pick argmin cost, with deterministic tie-breaks.

### Hysteresis / anti-thrash
- Only switch if (cost_best + switch_margin) < cost_current.
- Default switch_margin = 0.05 * cost_current.
- Optional min_hold_frames = 5: keep current strategy for N frames unless improvement > 10%.
- If uncertainty high, prefer the safer strategy with lower worst-case cost:
  - Use q95 for costs, or
  - Pin to last stable strategy for one frame.

### Deterministic tie-breaks (in order)
1. Lower expected cost (strict).
2. Lower worst-case cost (q95).
3. Lower scan count (N_scan).
4. Prefer strategies in order: Spans > DirtyRows > Full > SAT > Redraw.
5. Stable strategy (last choice).

### Config defaults
- prior_alpha=1.0, prior_beta=19.0, decay=0.95
- conservative=false, conservative_quantile=0.95
- min_observation_cells=0
- switch_margin=0.05, min_hold_frames=5
- c_scan=1.0, c_emit=6.0, c_row=0.1
- c_span_overhead=c_row * D (row bookkeeping)
- c_sat_build=c_scan, c_tile=0.02*c_scan

### Determinism notes
- All inputs are derived from buffer sizes, dirty tracking, and counts.
- Tie-breaks are total-order (no randomness).
- If a strategy is unavailable (feature off), exclude from candidate set.

### Evidence log fields (for bd-3e1t.8.2+)
- alpha, beta, p_mean, p_var, p_q95
- n_scanned, k_changed
- cost_full, cost_dirty, cost_span, cost_sat, cost_redraw
- chosen_strategy, tie_break_reason, switch_margin","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberBay","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:53:50.996196285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:21:13.500628927Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:21:13.500604732Z","close_reason":"Spec drafted","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:53:50.996196285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-6qxh","title":"Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation","description":"# Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation

## Background & Motivation

Many widgets are static most of the time:
- Headers and footers
- Borders and decorations
- Rarely-changing status bars

Re-rendering them every frame wastes CPU.

**The solution:**
- Cache rendered widget output
- Invalidate on model change or resize
- Composite cached content with live content

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-10i.3.6 (Buffer with scissor/opacity stacks)
- Complements bd-1iqn (Render Budget)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct CachedWidget<W: Widget> {
    inner: W,
    cache: Option<CachedBuffer>,
    last_area: Option<Rect>,
    dirty: bool,
}

pub struct CachedBuffer {
    buffer: Buffer,
    hash: u64,  // Content hash for change detection
}

impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    pub fn new(widget: W) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self) { self.dirty = true; }
    
    pub fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let needs_render = self.dirty 
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.is_none();
            
        if needs_render {
            let mut cache_buf = Buffer::empty(area);
            self.inner.render(area, &mut cache_buf);
            self.cache = Some(CachedBuffer::new(cache_buf));
            self.last_area = Some(area);
            self.dirty = false;
        }
        
        if let Some(cache) = &self.cache {
            buf.merge(&cache.buffer);
        }
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Cache hit (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(widget = name, "Cache hit, using cached buffer");

// Cache miss (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, reason = ?reason, "Cache miss, re-rendering");

// Cache invalidated (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, "Cache invalidated via mark_dirty()");
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Test cache hit rate
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-stats --duration 10s --static-widgets 5
# Assert: cache hit rate > 95%

# Test memory bounds
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-memory --cached-widgets 100
# Assert: memory < 10MB for 100 widgets

# Verify correctness
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-verify --compare-to-uncached
# Assert: output identical
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CachedWidget<W> wrapper implemented
- [ ] Caching works for static widgets
- [ ] Invalidation on mark_dirty()
- [ ] Resize triggers re-render
- [ ] Memory usage bounded
- [ ] Content hash detects changes
- [ ] Integration with widget tree
- [ ] No stale content ever displayed
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Cache Safety Guidelines

### When to Use CachedWidget
- Static content (headers, footers, borders)
- Content that changes infrequently
- Expensive rendering operations

### When NOT to Use CachedWidget
- Dynamic content (logs, timers, animations)
- Content that depends on external state
- Small/fast widgets (caching overhead > render cost)

### Cache Coherence
The cache can become stale if:
1. Widget props change without mark_dirty()
2. External state affects rendering
3. Theme/style changes globally

Solution: Use automatic dirty detection via content hash:
```rust
impl<W: Widget + Hash> CachedWidget<W> {
    fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let current_hash = self.inner.content_hash();
        
        let needs_render = self.dirty
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.hash) != Some(current_hash);
        
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Memory Management
```rust
impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    /// Drop cache to free memory
    pub fn clear_cache(&mut self) {
        self.cache = None;
    }
    
    /// Check cache memory usage
    pub fn cache_size(&self) -> usize {
        self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.buffer.memory_size()).unwrap_or(0)
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-7nm3","title":"Virtualized List: Height Prediction (Bayes + Conformal Bounds)","description":"## Goal
Predict unseen row heights to avoid scroll jumps and reflow hitches, with formal uncertainty bounds.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Use a Bayesian online model for row height given metadata (item type, text length, wrapping). Maintain posterior for mean/variance and use conformal prediction to bound actual height:
- Posterior: h_i ~ Normal(mu_i, sigma_i^2) with conjugate updates.
- Conformal interval: [q_alpha_lower, q_alpha_upper] from calibration residuals.

Use bounds to pre-allocate scroll space; adjust only when actual height exits interval.

 {"id":"bd-7u8","title":"Implement cursor save/restore strategy (DEC vs ANSI vs emulated)","description":"# Implement Cursor Save/Restore Strategy

## Background
Plan Section 9.3 describes a critical cursor save/restore strategy for inline mode robustness. Terminals differ in which save/restore sequences they support reliably.

## Problem Statement
Inline mode requires saving cursor position before drawing UI and restoring after. However:
- DEC save/restore (\`ESC 7\` / \`ESC 8\`) is preferred but not universal
- ANSI save/restore (\`CSI s\` / \`CSI u\`) is an alternative
- Some terminals/muxes have quirks with either
- Must handle failures gracefully

## Layered Strategy (from Plan)

### Layer 1: DEC Save/Restore (Preferred)
\`\`\`rust
const DEC_SAVE: &[u8] = b"\x1b7";    // ESC 7 (DECSC)
const DEC_RESTORE: &[u8] = b"\x1b8"; // ESC 8 (DECRC)
\`\`\`
- Most widely supported on modern terminals
- Saves more state (cursor position, attributes, charset)
- Works in tmux/screen with passthrough

### Layer 2: ANSI Save/Restore (Fallback)
\`\`\`rust
const ANSI_SAVE: &[u8] = b"\x1b[s";    // CSI s
const ANSI_RESTORE: &[u8] = b"\x1b[u"; // CSI u
\`\`\`
- Less widely supported
- Only saves cursor position (not attributes)
- May conflict with some terminal modes

### Layer 3: Emulated (Last Resort)
\`\`\`rust
// Track cursor position explicitly
struct CursorTracker {
    x: u16,
    y: u16,
}

impl CursorTracker {
    fn save(&mut self, current_x: u16, current_y: u16) {
        self.x = current_x;
        self.y = current_y;
    }
    
    fn restore(&self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        write!(writer, "\x1b[{};{}H", self.y + 1, self.x + 1)
    }
}
\`\`\`
- Works everywhere that supports CUP
- Requires tracking cursor position throughout
- More overhead but guaranteed to work

## Strategy Selection
\`\`\`rust
pub enum CursorSaveStrategy {
    Dec,       // ESC 7 / ESC 8
    Ansi,      // CSI s / CSI u
    Emulated,  // Track and CUP
    Auto,      // Detect best for environment
}

impl CursorSaveStrategy {
    pub fn detect(caps: &TerminalCapabilities) -> Self {
        // Most terminals support DEC
        if !caps.in_screen {
            return Self::Dec;
        }
        // Screen has quirks, try ANSI
        Self::Ansi
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation
\`\`\`rust
pub struct CursorManager {
    strategy: CursorSaveStrategy,
    saved_position: Option<(u16, u16)>,  // For emulated mode
}

impl CursorManager {
    pub fn save(&mut self, writer: &mut impl Write, current_pos: (u16, u16)) -> io::Result<()> {
        match self.strategy {
            CursorSaveStrategy::Dec => writer.write_all(DEC_SAVE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Ansi => writer.write_all(ANSI_SAVE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Emulated => {
                self.saved_position = Some(current_pos);
                Ok(())
            }
            CursorSaveStrategy::Auto => {
                // Try DEC, it's most common
                writer.write_all(DEC_SAVE)
            }
        }
    }
    
    pub fn restore(&mut self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        match self.strategy {
            CursorSaveStrategy::Dec => writer.write_all(DEC_RESTORE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Ansi => writer.write_all(ANSI_RESTORE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Emulated => {
                if let Some((x, y)) = self.saved_position {
                    write!(writer, "\x1b[{};{}H", y + 1, x + 1)?;
                }
                Ok(())
            }
            CursorSaveStrategy::Auto => writer.write_all(DEC_RESTORE),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

Test that verifies cursor is correctly restored:
1. Move cursor to known position
2. Save cursor
3. Move cursor elsewhere
4. Restore cursor
5. Query position (DSR) and verify matches saved position
